verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: Jovian having returned from Persia, ecclesiastical commotions were again renewed: for those who presided over the churches endeavored to anticipate each other, in the hope that the emperor would attach himself to their own tenets.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: He however had from the beginning adhered to the homoousian faith, and openly declared that he preferred this to all others.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: Moreover, he wrote letters to and encouraged Athanasius bishop of Alexandria, who immediately after Julian’s death had recovered the Alexandrian church, and at that time gaining confidence from the letters [spoken of] put away all fear.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: The emperor further recalled from exile all those prelates whom Constantius had banished, and who had not been re-established by Julian.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: Moreover, the pagan temples were again shut up, and they secreted themselves wherever they were able.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: The philosophers also laid aside their palliums, and clothed themselves in ordinary attire.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: That public pollution by the blood of victims, which had been profusely lavished even to disgust in the reign of Julian, was now likewise taken away.
